verification.regression_notes: Retrospective-only output/status/state updates; no product runtime changes.

handoff.summary: Retrospective completed with 5 action items. All 6 A-series prompts completed successfully. One B-series bug fix (temp file collisions) was required. 3,526 lines added across 22 files. 9/9 acceptance criteria verified at V1 gate.
next_steps: Open PR from feature/extension-agent-integration to main and merge.
After merge, update workpack state from review to complete.
Track action items AI-1 through AI-5 in the next planning cycle.
known_issues: workpack.state.json was not updated per-prompt during execution — all prompt statuses remained 'pending' until retrospective.
Execution telemetry fields (tokens_in, tokens_out, duration_ms) are zeroed — no automatic metric collection yet.
Earlier branch attempt (edc5c86, 6f2bcc1) was abandoned and rebased, creating parallel commit history.

notes:
1. Summary
- Workpack: 03_workpack-manager_agent-integration
- Duration: 2026-02-23 (planning) → 2026-02-26 (implementation + verification), ~46 min active implementation (15:29–16:15 UTC+1)
- Agents Used: GitHub Copilot (Claude Opus 4.6) for all prompts
- Total Prompts: 7 (A0–A5 + R1)
- Status: Complete

2. What went well
- All 6 A-series prompts completed in a single implementation session (~46 minutes wall-clock).
- DAG parallelization worked correctly: A2, A3, and A4 were executed in parallel after A1 (Phase 2), matching the plan.
- Clean TypeScript compilation throughout — zero tsc errors at every stage.
- 29/29 tests passing with zero regressions on the existing test suite; agent-specific tests added 1,406 lines of test coverage.
- A5 verification gate confirmed 9/9 acceptance criteria met on first run.
- Provider interface design proved extensible: MockProvider test demonstrates adding a new provider without modifying core code.
- SecretStorage-based credential handling for CodexProvider avoided any hardcoded secrets.
- Architecture cleanly separates types.ts (interface), registry.ts (discovery), assignment.ts (mapping), dag.ts (dependency), and orchestrator.ts (execution).

3. What caused friction
- Atomic state-write temp file collisions: parallel orchestrator dispatches caused race conditions in saveWorkpackStateAtomic, requiring a bug fix commit (6b5c4b5).
- workpack.state.json was never updated during execution — all prompt statuses remained 'pending'. The state file should have been updated after each prompt completion.
- An earlier branch attempt (edc5c86, 6f2bcc1) was abandoned before the current clean implementation, creating redundant commit history.
- Execution telemetry (tokens, duration) is not captured — all output JSONs have zeroed telemetry fields, limiting estimation calibration.
- The workpack meta says created_at 2026-02-23 but all implementation happened on 2026-02-26, creating a 3-day gap between planning and execution.

4. Protocol observations
- workpack.meta.json: Valuable for defining the prompt DAG and effort estimates. The parallelization map (A2/A3/A4 after A1) was directly derived from it.
- workpack.state.json: Added value in theory but was not maintained during execution — state drifted from reality. Needs enforcement.
- Prompt DAG: Respected and useful. The dependency ordering (A0→A1→{A2,A3,A4}→A5→R1) was natural and prevented ordering mistakes.
- Execution boundaries: Clear for this workpack — agent handled all code generation; orchestrator (human) handled commit strategy and verification.
- Protocol improvement suggestions: (1) Enforce state.json updates via post-prompt hooks. (2) Add automatic telemetry capture. (3) Add commit SHA tracking per-prompt output.

5. Agent provider feedback
- Provider: GitHub Copilot (Claude Opus 4.6)
- Prompts handled: A0, A1, A2, A3, A4, A5, R1 (all)
- Quality: 4/5
- Strengths: Fast execution, correct TypeScript output, good test coverage, proper separation of concerns.
- Weaknesses: Did not update workpack.state.json per-prompt; temp file collision bug required a B-series fix; abandoned first branch attempt.
- Would reassign: No — overall high quality delivery with minor process gaps.

6. Metrics
| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Total prompts | 7 |
| Completed on first try | 5 (A0, A1, A2, A3, A5) |
| Required rework | 1 (A4 — temp file collision bug fix) |
| Blocked prompts | 0 |
| Average prompt duration | ~7 min (46 min / 6 implementation prompts) |
| Total workpack duration | 46 min active implementation |
| Lines added | 3,526 |
| Lines removed | 15 |
| Files changed | 22 |
| Tests added | 1,406 lines across 6 test files |
| Test pass rate | 100% (29/29) |
| AC coverage | 9/9 |

7. Action items
- AI-1: Enforce workpack.state.json updates after each prompt completion (post-prompt hook or agent rule). Owner: AGENT_RULES.md maintainers. Priority: P1.
- AI-2: Add automatic execution telemetry capture (tokens_in, tokens_out, duration_ms) to output JSON generation. Owner: tooling maintainers. Priority: P2.
- AI-3: Add commit SHA tracking per-prompt (each prompt commits its own deliverables before writing output JSON). Owner: AGENT_RULES.md maintainers. Priority: P1.
- AI-4: Add temp file uniqueness to atomic state-write utility to prevent parallel dispatch collisions. Owner: extension maintainers. Priority: P2. Status: DONE (6b5c4b5).
- AI-5: Recalibrate M-series effort estimates: A1 (M planned, ~35s actual), A2/A3 (M planned, <1m actual). Current M estimates are significantly over-estimated for agent-driven code generation. Owner: workpack planners. Priority: P3.
